title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Full Investigation & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the complete lifecycle of FIX-467, a staging environment bug where creating the first library ticket produced no artifacts after several hours. The root cause was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully resolve this issue. The tour follows the investigation in data-flow order: from the initial user report, through successive agent triage runs, to the final corrected token-selection logic.

steps.description: This is the entry point: the original ticket FIX-467 filed by the user. The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours. Screenshots were attached as evidence of the failure. A critical constraint is noted: the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This file seeds the entire investigation and scopes the problem to the staging artifact creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run focused on understanding the failure mode from the attached screenshots and available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping what part of the artifact creation pipeline is broken.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Investigation (Run 2)

steps.description: By this third run, the agent believed it had found and fixed the issue — reporting 'Your changes are ready! Updated 1 repository' and providing verification screenshots. However, the user (Usher) followed up at 2026-05-15T03:30:52Z clarifying the actual root cause: the system was using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories for NetSuite file cabinet repos. This run is critical because it reveals that the first fix resolved a surface symptom, not the underlying token-selection logic.
steps.title: Step 3: First Fix Attempt and User Escalation (Run 3)

steps.description: This run represents the agent's deepened understanding of the root cause after the user's clarification. The agent explicitly confirmed that the token-selection logic — not just surface-level error handling — needed to be corrected. For NetSuite file cabinet repos specifically, repository creation must use the built-in Helix system token, never the user's personal authentication token. This run drove the investigation into the token-selection code path and prepared the corrective changes.
steps.title: Step 4: Root Cause Identified — Wrong Token for Repo Creation (Run 4)

steps.description: This is the first-generation CodeTour walkthrough generated after the token root cause was identified. It documents the investigation history and begins mapping the fix rationale. Note that this version was superseded by later, more complete walkthroughs as the fix was verified and refined across additional runs. Reading this file alongside the later versions illustrates how the agent's understanding of the problem evolved.
steps.title: Step 5: First-Generation CodeTour (Run 4 Intraview)

steps.description: This second-generation CodeTour walkthrough was produced in a subsequent run after the token-selection fix was applied. It refines the narrative from Run 4's walkthrough, adding clearer data-flow descriptions for how the Helix system token is selected and passed during repository creation. This version includes more precise step descriptions and corrects gaps in the earlier tour's coverage of the NetSuite file cabinet repo code path.
steps.title: Step 6: Refined CodeTour After Token Fix Applied (Run 5 Intraview)

steps.description: This is the final, most complete CodeTour walkthrough, generated in the last run after full verification. It consolidates the investigation history, root cause explanation, and fix rationale into a single authoritative tour. The description confirms the fix: repository creation for NetSuite file cabinet repos now correctly uses the built-in Helix system token, not the user's token. This file is the canonical reference for understanding what FIX-467 changed and why.
steps.title: Step 7: Final Verified CodeTour — Canonical Reference (Run 6 Intraview)

steps.description: This is the final ticket context file, capturing the full discussion thread from initial report through resolution. It documents the complete data flow of the investigation: user report → agent triage (multiple runs) → surface fix → user clarification of root cause → token-selection logic fix → verification. With this run, the bug is resolved: the first library ticket creation in staging now succeeds because the system correctly routes NetSuite file cabinet repo creation through the Helix system token rather than the user's personal token. All major code paths for artifact creation remain intact; only the token-selection branch for this specific repo type was corrected.
steps.title: Step 8: Summary — Full Ticket Resolution and Clean Code Paths
